summary: theta-kit postinstall fetches and executes remote JavaScript from anonymous paste host
details: package.json declares `postinstall: node index.js`, which loads lib/index.js. At line 7-12, lib/index.js axios-GETs a base64-obfuscated URL that decodes to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/2P5FA (an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host), reads `.data.cookie` from the response, spawns a detached `node` child process with `stdio: ['pipe','ignore','ignore']`, and writes the fetched string to its stdin — executing attacker-controlled JavaScript on the installer's machine at npm-install time. The URL is obfuscated via base64 (`atob(DEV_API_KEY)`), there is no hash or signature verification on the fetched payload, the source host is author-mutable, and the child process is detached and silenced (stderr/stdout ignored). This is a textbook install-time dropper: any consumer running `npm install theta-kit` runs whatever JavaScript the operator of the paste currently serves.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
